subject: Q1 Cocoa Pricing + Delivery Schedule
body:
Hi Amara,

Hope you're well. Can you send over your Q1 pricing for the standard grade cocoa, and confirm the delivery window we discussed for the February shipment (40 tons)?

Thanks,
David

subject: RE: Q1 Cocoa Pricing + Delivery Schedule
body:
Hello David, sorry for delay, was traveling.

Q1 price is $2,850/ton for standard grade. Please note that all pricing from Ivoire Cacao Export is denominated in USD. The exchange rate applied to your invoice is the prevailing rate on the date of invoice issuance, not the date of order.

For delivery — our standard dispatch-to-arrival window is 14 business days from order confirmation. Please check with Jean-Baptiste in operations for the specific schedule, he handles logistics. Let me know if you want to proceed and I send formal quote letter.

Best,
Amara

subject: Delivery confirmation - Feb shipment (40t)
body:
Hi Jean-Baptiste,

Amara mentioned you handle logistics. We're confirming the Q1 order — 40 tons standard grade. She says the standard window is 14 business days from order confirmation. We need the shipment on site before February 20 for production planning. Can you confirm this is achievable and send a schedule?

Thanks,
David

subject: Re: Delivery confirmation - Feb shipment (40t)
body:
David

port congestion abidjan this month, vessel delay. we are now tracking approximately 26 business days from order confirmation date due to the situation at port. realistic arrival now late february or early march. will update closer.

sorry

jb

subject: RE: Delivery — this is a problem
body:
JB,

Amara confirmed 14 business days at order. We are now looking at 26 business days — that is nearly double the committed window and it puts us past our production deadline.

I need a firm updated date this week. Is there any option to expedite — partial shipment, alternative vessel?

David

subject: RE: delivery update
body:
David, I understand. I spoke with JB. The port situation is outside our control but I know it does not help you.

He says earliest realistic vessel now is February 10 departure from Abidjan, so arrival at your port around March 5-10. No option to split — the batch is one lot.

Amara

subject: Following up - shipment status
body:
JB,

We are past the March 5-10 window and I have no update. This is now six weeks past the original 14-business-day commitment. Can you give me a firm date this week?

David

subject: Updated pricing for upcoming order
body:
Hi David, hope all is good.

Quick note — cocoa market has moved a lot since January. For any new order after this batch the price will be $3,050/ton, reflecting current ICCO benchmark plus our margin. Let me know if you want to lock the current order at old price before it ships.

Amara

subject: Re: Updated pricing + shipment still missing
body:
Amara,

The current order hasn't even shipped yet. JB said early March and we're still waiting. Can we get clarity on both — when does this order actually arrive, and is the new pricing only for future orders or does it touch what is already contracted?

David

subject: shipment update
body:
david

container left port last week, shd reach you mid april. note final loaded qty was 38.5t not 40t — two pallets failed quality check at our end before loading. will credit difference on next invoice.

jb

subject: Re: shipment arrived — delivery confirmed
body:
JB,

Confirming 38.5t arrived today and passed incoming inspection. Quality is fine.

On the short shipment — you mentioned a credit on the next invoice for the 1.5t shortfall. At Q1 contract price of $2,850/ton that is $4,275. Please make sure this appears on the next invoice.

Also — Amara mentioned $3,050/ton for Q2. Our contract (signed June 2025) sets a base price with a quarterly review mechanism — price adjustments require written confirmation from both sides. Neither the January quote ($2,850) nor the proposed Q2 quote has gone through that process. I need a formal written price review request before we can agree to anything above contract base.

David

subject: Q2 order + credit
body:
Hi David,

Glad shipment arrived ok. Credit of $4,275 will appear on the Q2 invoice, Jean-Baptiste will raise it.

For Q2 — yes, new price is $3,050/ton. This is not negotiable, the market is where it is. When do you want to place Q2 order? We need at least 6 weeks lead time.

Amara

subject: RE: Q2 order — formal price review required
body:
Amara,

I need to be clear. Our contract sets a base price of $2,650/ton with a quarterly review mechanism. Price adjustments require written confirmation from both parties. Neither the $2,850 Q1 rate nor the $3,050 Q2 proposal has gone through that process formally.

I am not saying we won't pay a fair market price. But we need to follow the contract process. Please send a formal written price review request with the ICCO benchmark reference so we can process this on our end.

David

subject: RE: Q2 order
body:
David,

Sorry for slow reply, I was at the Salon du Cacao in Paris.

I understand what you say about the contract process. Honestly I do not have a copy of the contract here — Jean-Baptiste handles administration. I will ask him to check and send something formal. But we cannot wait too long, the Q2 harvest window is short.

Amara

subject: Q2 — still waiting on formal price review
body:
Amara, JB,

It has been three weeks since I asked for a formal price review request and I have not received anything. We want to place the Q2 order — 35-40 tons — but I cannot authorize payment above the contract base price without a written amendment.

If I do not hear back with the formal documentation by June 10, we will either place at contract price or pause until we can align.

David
